name: EAEU Treaty 2014 (Eurasian Economic Union)
summary: The Treaty on the Eurasian Economic Union (EAEU Treaty) is a regional supranational meta-framework foundational-EAEU-treaty framework adopted by the 3 founding Russia + Kazakhstan + Belarus states in Astana — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern post-Soviet regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal post-Soviet integration framework + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Russia-led-economic-integration framework (EAEU Treaty signed 29 May 2014 in Astana Kazakhstan by Belarus + Kazakhstan + Russia + effective 1 January 2015 + Eurasian Customs Union ECU predecessor founded 1 January 2010 + Eurasian Economic Community EurAsEC predecessor founded 10 October 2000 — formal foundational EAEU treaty framework establishing comprehensive EAEU organisation framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-regional-treaty frameworks which operate on different geographic scopes, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern post-Soviet regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal post-Soviet integration framework + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Russia-led-economic-integration framework + EAEU operating formal customs-union + common-market + planned-monetary-union framework + ~3.2 trillion combined GDP framework + ~180 million population framework + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal post-Russia-invasion-of-Ukraine western-sanctions-encirclement framework + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal CSTO-collective-security-overlap framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal EAEU Court framework (Court of the Eurasian Economic Union established 1 January 2015 in Minsk Belarus providing formal EAEU judicial framework with individual-petition framework via Article 39 EAEU Treaty for natural + legal persons — formal foundational EAEU judicial framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern post-Soviet regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal individual-petition court framework + first modern post-Soviet regional supranational court operating formal individual-petition framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal EAEU Customs Union + Common Market framework (EAEU Customs Union effective from EurAsEC 1 January 2010 + EAEU Common Market effective from 1 January 2012 providing formal four-freedoms framework: free movement of goods + services + capital + labour — formal foundational EAEU customs-union + common-market framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern post-Soviet regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal customs-union + common-market framework + only modern post-Soviet regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal four-freedoms framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal EAEU Free Trade Agreements + Greater Eurasian Partnership framework (EAEU Free Trade Agreements with Vietnam 2015 + Singapore 2019 + Serbia 2019 + Iran 2018 Interim Agreement upgraded 2023 Free Trade Agreement + China Trade and Economic Cooperation Agreement 2018 + Greater Eurasian Partnership framework — formal foundational EAEU Free Trade Agreements + Greater Eurasian Partnership framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern post-Soviet regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal EAEU Free Trade Agreements + Greater Eurasian Partnership framework). The EAEU Treaty is binding upon 5 EAEU member states as of 2026 (Armenia + Belarus + Kazakhstan + Kyrgyzstan + Russia) + 3 observer states Cuba + Moldova + Uzbekistan — comprehensive post-Soviet regional coverage framework. The Treaty is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal EAEU four-freedoms framework operates substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal post-Soviet cross-border-children's-rights framework + free-movement-of-labour framework. The EAEU Supreme Eurasian Economic Council + EAEU Eurasian Intergovernmental Council + EAEU Eurasian Economic Commission EEC + EAEU Court are the formal EAEU bodies; national-level apex courts operate as judicial bodies. The EAEU Treaty is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.title: EurAsEC
statutory_framework.relevance: Eurasian Economic Community EurAsEC founded 10 October 2000 in Astana Kazakhstan by Belarus + Kazakhstan + Kyrgyzstan + Russia + Tajikistan — predecessor framework to EAEU.

statutory_framework.title: ECU
statutory_framework.relevance: Eurasian Customs Union ECU founded 1 January 2010 by Belarus + Kazakhstan + Russia — predecessor framework to EAEU.

statutory_framework.title: EAEU Treaty
statutory_framework.relevance: Treaty on the Eurasian Economic Union signed 29 May 2014 in Astana Kazakhstan + effective 1 January 2015 — formal foundational EAEU treaty framework establishing comprehensive EAEU organisation framework.

statutory_framework.citation: EAEU Treaty Article 39 (EAEU Court Individual-Petition Framework)
statutory_framework.title: EAEU Treaty Article 39
statutory_framework.relevance: EAEU Treaty Article 39 framework on EAEU Court individual-petition framework for natural + legal persons — formal foundational EAEU judicial framework.

statutory_framework.title: EAEU-Iran FTA
statutory_framework.relevance: EAEU-Iran Free Trade Agreement signed 25 December 2023 in Saint Petersburg Russia + effective 15 May 2025 — formal EAEU Free Trade Agreement framework.

apex_courts.name: EAEU Supreme Eurasian Economic Council
apex_courts.role: Formal supreme decision-making body for EAEU.

apex_courts.name: Court of the Eurasian Economic Union
apex_courts.role: Apex judicial body for EAEU framework with individual-petition framework via Article 39 EAEU Treaty for natural + legal persons + first modern post-Soviet regional supranational court operating formal individual-petition framework.

anonymisation_convention: EAEU-related communications via EAEU Court framework operate formal anonymisation convention per EAEU Court Rules of Procedure on individual-petition framework.

key_developments.title: CIS Commonwealth of Independent States established
key_developments.description: Commonwealth of Independent States CIS established 8 December 1991 in Belovezha Belarus following dissolution of Soviet Union — predecessor framework to EurAsEC + EAEU.

key_developments.title: EurAsEC founded
key_developments.description: Eurasian Economic Community EurAsEC founded 10 October 2000 in Astana Kazakhstan — predecessor framework to EAEU.

key_developments.title: Eurasian Customs Union founded
key_developments.description: Eurasian Customs Union ECU founded 1 January 2010 by Belarus + Kazakhstan + Russia — predecessor framework to EAEU.

key_developments.title: EAEU Common Economic Space
key_developments.description: EAEU Common Economic Space effective from 1 January 2012 — predecessor framework to EAEU Common Market.

key_developments.title: EAEU Treaty adopted
key_developments.description: Treaty on the Eurasian Economic Union signed 29 May 2014 in Astana Kazakhstan — formal foundational EAEU treaty framework adopted.

key_developments.title: EAEU Treaty enters force + Armenia + Kyrgyzstan join
key_developments.description: EAEU Treaty entered force 1 January 2015 + Armenia joined 2 January 2015 + Kyrgyzstan joined 12 August 2015 + EAEU Court operational 1 January 2015 in Minsk — formal foundational EAEU treaty framework operational.

key_developments.title: EAEU-Vietnam FTA
key_developments.description: EAEU-Vietnam Free Trade Agreement signed 29 May 2015 + effective 5 October 2016 — formal foundational EAEU first Free Trade Agreement framework.

key_developments.title: Russia invades Ukraine + Western sanctions encirclement
key_developments.description: Russia invaded Ukraine 24 February 2022 + comprehensive Western sanctions imposed on Russia + Belarus + EAEU-internal trade framework expansion in response — formal EAEU Western-sanctions-encirclement framework operational.

key_developments.title: EAEU-Iran Free Trade Agreement signed
key_developments.description: EAEU-Iran Free Trade Agreement signed 25 December 2023 in Saint Petersburg Russia upgrading 2018 Interim Agreement — formal EAEU Free Trade Agreement framework.

key_developments.title: EAEU-Iran FTA enters force + Greater Eurasian Partnership 2025
key_developments.description: EAEU-Iran FTA effective 15 May 2025 + Greater Eurasian Partnership framework adopted 2025 — formal EAEU Free Trade Agreement + Greater Eurasian Partnership framework operational.

structural_findings: EAEU Treaty operates a regional supranational binding-treaty framework — places EAEU Treaty in the regional supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern post-Soviet regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal post-Soviet integration framework + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Russia-led-economic-integration framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern post-Soviet regional supranational binding-treaty framework + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Russia-led-economic-integration framework + ~3.2 trillion combined GDP + ~180 million population + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal post-Russia-invasion-of-Ukraine western-sanctions-encirclement framework + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal CSTO-collective-security-overlap framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal EAEU Court framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern post-Soviet regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal individual-petition court framework + first modern post-Soviet regional supranational court operating formal individual-petition framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal EAEU Customs Union + Common Market framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern post-Soviet regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal customs-union + common-market framework + only modern post-Soviet regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal four-freedoms framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal EAEU Free Trade Agreements + Greater Eurasian Partnership framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern post-Soviet regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal EAEU Free Trade Agreements + Greater Eurasian Partnership framework.
5 EAEU member state + 3 observer state ratification framework is structurally distinctive globally — comprehensive post-Soviet regional coverage framework.

1991 CIS + 2000 EurAsEC + 2010 ECU + 2012 Common Economic Space + 2014 EAEU Treaty + 2015 entry into force + Armenia + Kyrgyzstan + Vietnam FTA + 2022 Russia invasion + sanctions encirclement + 2023 Iran FTA + 2025 Iran FTA effective + Greater Eurasian Partnership EAEU-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.

EAEU Treaty + Article 39 individual-petition + Customs Union + Common Market + Vietnam + Iran + China FTAs + Greater Eurasian Partnership formal nonet framework with formal post-Soviet cross-border-children's-rights PA-intersection framework via four-freedoms + free-movement-of-labour framework is structurally distinctive globally.
editorial_notes: EAEU Treaty jurisdiction sidecar — regional supranational post-Soviet foundational-EAEU-treaty meta-framework (EAEU Treaty 2014/2015 + EurAsEC 2000 predecessor + ECU 2010 + Common Economic Space 2012 + Article 39 EAEU Court individual-petition + EAEU Court Minsk + Vietnam FTA 2015 + Iran FTA 2023/2025 + Singapore + Serbia 2019 + China Trade Agreement 2018 + 2022 Russia invasion + Western sanctions encirclement + 2025 Greater Eurasian Partnership). Only modern post-Soviet regional supranational binding-treaty framework globally + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Russia-led-economic-integration framework + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal post-Russia-invasion-of-Ukraine western-sanctions-encirclement framework + central jurisdiction of formal EAEU Court individual-petition framework + first modern post-Soviet regional supranational court individual-petition + central jurisdiction of formal Customs Union + Common Market four-freedoms + central jurisdiction of formal EAEU Free Trade Agreements + Greater Eurasian Partnership framework + 5 EAEU member states (Armenia + Belarus + Kazakhstan + Kyrgyzstan + Russia) + 3 observer states (Cuba + Moldova + Uzbekistan) + 1991 CIS + 2000 EurAsEC + 2010 ECU + 2012 Common Economic Space + 2014 EAEU Treaty + 2015 entry into force + Armenia + Kyrgyzstan + Vietnam FTA + 2022 Russia invasion + sanctions + 2023 Iran FTA + 2025 Iran FTA effective + Greater Eurasian Partnership EAEU-framework-development sequence.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator. NOTE: EAEU four-freedoms framework operates substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal post-Soviet cross-border-children's-rights framework + free-movement-of-labour framework.

Joins regional + supranational + post-Soviet-foundational-EAEU-treaty cluster + EAEU-Treaty-only-post-Soviet-regional-supranational-binding-treaty-globally-distinctive + Russia-led-economic-integration + 3.2-trillion-combined-GDP + 180-million-population + post-Russia-invasion-of-Ukraine-Western-sanctions-encirclement + CSTO-collective-security-overlap + EAEU-Court-Minsk-Article-39-individual-petition + first-modern-post-Soviet-regional-supranational-court-individual-petition + EAEU-Customs-Union-2010 + EAEU-Common-Market-2012 + four-freedoms-goods-services-capital-labour + EAEU-Vietnam-FTA-2015-first-Free-Trade-Agreement + EAEU-Iran-FTA-2023-effective-2025 + EAEU-Singapore-Serbia-2019 + EAEU-China-Trade-Agreement-2018 + Greater-Eurasian-Partnership-2025 + 5-EAEU-member-states + 3-EAEU-observer-states-Cuba-Moldova-Uzbekistan + 1991-CIS-predecessor + 2000-EurAsEC-predecessor + post-Soviet-cross-border-children-rights-PA-intersection-via-four-freedoms clusters within the corpus.
